title: Bernard Arnault's succession, the elephant in the room at LVMH
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (6/6). The head of the luxury group deliberately raised his five children so that each one could potentially succeed him. While the family presents a united front, the struggle for succession continues behind the scenes.

title: Over 100,000 flee or are ordered indoors as wildfires rage in France and Spain
Bab Al-Mandab Strait threatens to be new front in war with Iran
Tariffs: Trump pursues his trade war through every method available
subhead: The US announced new tariffs on imports from 60 economies, ranging from 10% to 12.5%, on Thursday, as previous measures were set to expire. These rules are set to remain in place for some time, pending possible legal challenges.

title: A show of strength from Ukrainian society
subhead: The spontaneous mobilization of Ukrainians, who took to the streets to voice their anger over the dismissal of their defense minister despite relentless Russian strikes, paid off. The country's cohesion ultimately prevailed, while a prolonged crisis would have weakened Ukraine's standing with its allies.

title: In Brittany, the delicious crêpes at the Kiosque de Mimi
subhead: 'Gourmet kiosks' (3/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. The Kiosque de Mimi in Vannes, a town in France's Brittany region, offers crêpes at any time of day.

title: OpenLux: How Luxembourg became China's financial gateway to Europe
subhead: Beijing has used Luxembourg to invest tens of billions of euros on a massive scale. By acquiring leading technology companies and strategic real estate, it has woven a network that challenges European sovereignty.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
subhead: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

title: How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

title: The profile of France's new defender of rights raises concern
